english: su č'ud-pa (su chud pa) = kóṅ-du č'ud-pa (khong du chud pa); t'ugs-su ᵒbyóṅ-pa (thugs su 'byong pa) to be kept in mind, in memory Mil.; also = yid-du ᵒóṅ-ba (yid du 'ong ba) ni f.; cf. ᵒgró-ba ('gro ba). — 3. t'ugs-la btágs-so (thugs la btags so) v. ᵒdógs-pa ('dogs pa). — 4. for t'ugs-rje (thugs rje) or bka - drín (bka' drin), t'ugs mdzád - pa (thugs mdzad pa) to grant or show a favour Dzl. — 5 in the phrase t'ugs mi t'úb-pa (thugs mi thub pa), with the genit. of the inf., it is used without ceremonial distinctions for to venture, to risk, to dare Dzl. Comp. t'ugs-ka (thugs ka) v. above — t'ugs - mk'yén (thugs mkhyen) resp. for mṅón - šés (mngon shes) Mil. — t'ugs - k'rúgs (thugs khrugs) resp. for koṅ - k'rúgs (khong khrugs) Ma. — t'ugs-dgóṅs (thugs dgongs) = dgóṅs-pa (dgongs pa) II.; t'ugs-dgóṅs gṭóṅ-ba (thugs dgongs gtong ba) = bsam-bló gtóṅ-ba (bsam blo gtong ba) to muse, meditate, reflect Mil. — t'úgs(-su) gró-ba (thugs su gro ba) resp. for yid-du ᵒóṅ-ba (yid du 'ong ba) to be agreeable; agreeable, pleasant, delightful; pleasure, delight, ... la (la) in (a thing) Mil. — t'ugs - rgyál (thugs rgyal) resp. anger, wrath, indignation Mil., t'ugs-rgyál bžeṅs (thugs rgyal bzhengs) anger arises, is roused. — t'ugs-ṅán (thugs ngan) grief, sorrow, affliction Dzl. — t'úgs-č'es-pa (thugs ches pa) resp. for yid-č'es-pa (yid ches pa) to believe. — t'úgs-rje (thugs rje) prop. respectful word for sñyíṅ-rje (snying rje) pity, commiseration, compassion; gen. grace, mercy, generosity, ṅa-la t'úgs-rje(s) gzigs (nga la thugs rjes gzigs) pray, look graciously upon me! Mil.; even thus: sá - bon žig t'úgs - rje gzigs dgos (sa bon zhig thugs rje gzigs dgos), pray, be so kind as to send me some seeds! W. — t'úgs - rjes ᵒdzín-pa (thugs rjes 'dzin pa), t'úgs - rje mdzád - pa (thugs rje mdzad pa) id. — t'ugs-rje - čan (thugs rje can) gracious, merciful, generous. — (lha) t'úgs - rje č'én - po (lha thugs rje chen po) the All - merciful, Awalokiteswara. — t'ugs-dám (thugs dam), prop. resp. for yi-dam (yi dam), 1. oath, vow, solemn promise, e.g. bčá - ba (bca' ba) to take (an oath), to make (a vow). 2. a prayer, a wish in the form of a prayer, = smóṅ-lam (smon lam). 3. contemplation, the act of contemplating a deity (cf. sgóm-pa (sgom pa) and sgrúb - pa (sgrub pa)); meditation in general, Mil. frq., t'ugs - dám ᵒp'el (thugs dam 'phel) meditation increases, proceeds successfully; devotion. 4. a deity, a tutelar god or saint, a patron Glr. — t'ugs - nyid (thugs nyid) v. sems - nyid (sems nyid), sub sems (sems). — t'ugs-múg (thugs mug) resp. for yi(d)-múg (yid mug) despair. — t'ugs bdé-ba (thugs bde ba), mi bdé-ba (mi bde ba), v. bdé-ba (bde ba). — t'ugs-gtsígs-pa (thugs gtsigs pa) to be cautious Sch.; v. however gtsígs-pa (gtsigs pa). — t'ugs-brtsé-ba (thugs brtse ba) love, affection of the heart, compassion, resp. for sñyíṅ-brtsé-ba (snying brtse ba), frq., t'ugs-brtse-bar dgóṅs-pa (thugs brtse bar dgongs pa), gzigs-pa (gzigs pa), with la (la), to look upon compassionately, to remember in mercy. — t'ugs-ráb (thugs rab) Sch. = šes-ráb (shes rab). — t'ugs-rús (thugs rus) Mil. = sñyiṅ-rús (snying rus). — t'ugs-(kyi) srás (thugs kyi sras) Mil., Tar., spiritual son, an appellation given to the most distinguished scholars of saints.
t'uṅ-ṅa (thung nga) three years old, of animals Sch.

col. t'uṅ-ṅu (thung ngu), Ld. *t'uṅ-se* (*thung se*), short, relative to space, time, quantity of vowels etc.; t'uṅ-ṅu ᵒgro-ba (thung ngu 'gro ba) to become shorter; but the word is not so much used as 'short' is in English; yid t'uṅ-ba (yid thung ba) Dzl., spro t'uṅ-ba (spro thung ba) Wdn. passionate, hot-tempered, hasty.
cheese made of buttermilk, or of č'úr-p'e (chur phe), butter and milk Ld., Glr., Pth.; ᵒó-t'ud ('o thud) milk-cheese, made of curd, or of milk coagulated with runnet.

I. a regular amount, a fixed quantity 1. of time, a certain length of time, as long as a man is able to work without resting, a shift, six, four, or three hours; Schf. translates Tar. 67, 17 even by one hour; a night-watch, mel-tse t'un k'or (mel tse thun khor) the night-watch is over Dzl.; t'un bžii rnal-byor (thun bzhi'i rnal 'byor) the meditation of a whole day Mil.; *t'un čad-če* (*thun cad ce*) W. (the cock) announces the watch (by crowing); t'un bzuṅ-ba (thun bzung ba) Pth. prob. to have the watch; nam-gyi guṅ-t'un-la (nam gyi gung thun la) at or about midnight; srod-kyi guṅ-t'un-la (srod kyi gung thun la) Mil. prob. id. — 2. a dose of medicine Med. frq. — tun-log? (thun log) II. in sorcery: bodies or substances which are supposed to be possessed of magic virtues, such as sand, barley, certain seeds etc., t'un-doṅ (thun dong) a hole in which such substances are concealed; t'un-rá (thun ra) a horn to carry them; t'un gsó-ba (thun gso ba) to revive a charm Mil. nt. III. one who collects, a gatherer (from ᵒt'ú-ba ('thu ba)), šiṅ-t'ún (shing thun) one who picks up or gathers sticks Mil.; rtsa-t'un (rtsa thun) a gatherer of grass, sñe-t'ún (snye thun) a gatherer of ears of corn Cs.; t'un-zór (thun zor) reaping-hook, sickle Sch. IV. t'un (thun), or more frq. t'un-móṅ(s) (thun mong(s)), usual,
